The repository at `/home/user/repo` has two workspaces, but the secondary workspace at `/home/user/secondary-ws` is stale.

Fix the stale workspace, verify both workspaces are healthy, and write a recovery status report to `/home/user/recovery_status.txt`.
Let me start by exploring both workspaces to understand their structure and identify what's stale about the secondary workspace.

parts.text: Both workspaces are healthy. Now let me write the recovery status report.

input.content:
================================================================================
WORKSPACE RECOVERY STATUS REPORT
Generated: Thu Mar 19 2026 07:23:51 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)
================================================================================

SUMMARY
-------
A stale working copy was detected in the secondary jj workspace at
/home/user/secondary-ws. The workspace was successfully recovered using
`jj workspace update-stale`. Both workspaces are now healthy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PRE-RECOVERY STATE
------------------
Source file: /home/user/workspace_status_before.txt

  default:   qqvxzskv e4211700 main | Add core module
  secondary: yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)

Secondary workspace stale error (before fix):
  Error: The working copy is stale (not updated since operation f6ad7cf4f7a7).
  Hint: Run `jj workspace update-stale` to update it.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RECOVERY ACTION
---------------
Command executed in /home/user/secondary-ws:
  $ jj workspace update-stale

Output:
  Working copy  (@) now at: yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)
  Parent commit (@-)      : pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
  Added 0 files, modified 0 files, removed 1 files
  Updated working copy to fresh commit 6cd7a48d77f9

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
POST-RECOVERY WORKSPACE HEALTH
-------------------------------

[DEFAULT WORKSPACE] /home/user/repo
  Status : HEALTHY
  Working copy (@) : qqvxzskv e4211700 main | Add core module
  Parent commit (@-): pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
  Uncommitted changes: core.py (Added)

[SECONDARY WORKSPACE] /home/user/secondary-ws
  Status : HEALTHY (stale flag resolved)
  Working copy (@) : yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)
  Parent commit (@-): pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
  Uncommitted changes: none

[SHARED WORKSPACE LIST] (visible from both workspaces)
  default:   qqvxzskv e4211700 main | Add core module
  secondary: yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)

[COMMIT GRAPH] (all commits)
  yoyvvqxp  (empty) (no description set)  — secondary@
  qqvxzskv  Add core module               — main, default@
  pzpsmrux  Initial project setup
  zzzzzzzz  root()

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
---------
Root cause: The secondary workspace's working copy had not been updated since
jj operation f6ad7cf4f7a7. This is a known jj condition that occurs when
operations are performed in another workspace (or directly on the repo) without
the affected workspace's working copy being refreshed. The working copy becomes
"stale" — its on-disk state no longer reflects the current operation log.

Resolution: `jj workspace update-stale` replays the latest operation log entry
against the working copy, bringing it back in sync. No commits or history were
lost; the change-id (yoyvvqxp) and commit-id (6cd7a48d) are unchanged.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RESULT: RECOVERY SUCCESSFUL — both workspaces are healthy.
================================================================================

input.result:
The stale secondary workspace has been fixed and both workspaces are verified healthy.

**Root cause:** The secondary jj workspace at `/home/user/secondary-ws` had a stale working copy — it had not been updated since operation `f6ad7cf4f7a7`, which had been applied from another workspace.

**Fix applied:**
```
cd /home/user/secondary-ws
jj workspace update-stale
```
This replayed the latest operation log entry against the working copy, resynchronising it without losing any commits or history.

**Post-recovery state:**

| Workspace | Path | Change ID | Commit ID | Status |
|-----------|------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `default` | `/home/user/repo` | `qqvxzskv` | `e4211700` | ✅ Healthy |
| `secondary` | `/home/user/secondary-ws` | `yoyvvqxp` | `6cd7a48d` | ✅ Healthy (stale resolved) |

A full recovery status report has been written to `/home/user/recovery_status.txt`, covering the pre-recovery error, the action taken, post-recovery workspace state, commit graph, and diagnosis.
